prompt:
LoRA wrapping of Falcon-7B fails with the following error.
```python
from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM

model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('tiiuae/falcon-7b-instruct',
                                              trust_remote_code=True)
lora = LoraConfig(r=16, lora_alpha=32, target_modules=['q_proj', 'v_proj'],
                  task_type='CAUSAL_LM')
model = get_peft_model(model, lora)
```

Traceback:
```
ValueError: Target modules ['q_proj', 'v_proj'] not found in the base model. Please check the target modules and try again.
```

criteria.description: Identifies the actual root cause, not a downstream symptom
Suggests a fix that would actually resolve it
Does not introduce a new bug in the fix
Explains why the bug happened (mechanistic understanding)

reference_solution:
Root cause: Falcon's attention block uses a single combined projection named `query_key_value` (a single linear layer that produces Q, K, V via channel slicing) rather than three separate `q_proj`/`k_proj`/`v_proj` linears that Llama-family models use. PEFT's target_modules string-match doesn't find anything named `q_proj` in the Falcon graph, so LoRA can't attach.

Fix: target Falcon's actual attention layer name:
```python
lora = LoraConfig(r=16, lora_alpha=32, target_modules=['query_key_value'],
                  task_type='CAUSAL_LM')
```
A portable alternative is `target_modules='all-linear'`, which makes PEFT introspect the model graph and adapt every nn.Linear; works regardless of naming convention but produces a larger adapter (~3-5x the size of attention-only).

Mechanism: PEFT identifies modules to wrap by walking the model graph and matching layer names against the configured target_modules list. Most LLM families ship distinct q/k/v linears (Llama, Mistral, Phi, Qwen) so 'q_proj'/'v_proj' works. Falcon, BLOOM, and a handful of others use the fused QKV-as-one-linear pattern - reducing matmul overhead but breaking name-based matchers. The 'all-linear' fallback was added in peft 0.7+ specifically to paper over this fragmentation: it dynamically discovers all nn.Linear modules in the model. The trade-off: more adapter parameters, slightly slower training, but architectural portability.
